metaTitle: EXIF Remover — Strip GPS & Metadata (Free, Private, No Upload)
metaDescription: Securely remove GPS location and hidden metadata from up to 20 photos at once. 100% private, local browser processing. Keep your original photo quality intact.
introSummary: Protect your digital footprint with hugdot's Batch EXIF Remover. Our professional-grade tool allows you to strip GPS coordinates, camera settings, and timestamps from up to 20 images simultaneously. By processing everything locally in your browser, hugdot ensures your sensitive location data never touches a server. Whether you're clearing home addresses from marketplace photos or stripping device info for professional privacy, our 'Zero-Upload' technology keeps you safe.

contextUsage.title: For Marketplaces & Classifieds
contextUsage.desc: Selling items online? Batch-process all your product photos to strip your home GPS coordinates before posting on Facebook or Craigslist.

contextUsage.title: For Social Media Privacy
contextUsage.desc: Safely share entire vacation albums. Remove precise tracking data from up to 20 photos in one go before uploading to public forums.

contextUsage.title: For Professional Portfolios
contextUsage.desc: Clean your professional shots by removing camera serial numbers, lens metadata, and edit history while preserving 100% image quality.

howto.description: Drag and drop your JPG, PNG, or WebP files. Since processing is 100% local, even high-res batches load and scan instantly.
hugdot automatically scans each file and highlights sensitive data like GPS locations or timestamps that need to be removed.
Click 'Save All Clean Images'. Our engine strips hidden headers from all files in parallel without re-compressing or touching your pixels.
Download your sanitized images individually or as a single, organized ZIP archive. Your original location data is now gone forever.

why: Parallel Batch Processing — Clean up to 20 images at once with our high-speed, concurrency-managed local engine.
100% Mathematical Privacy — Your photos never leave your device. No cloud, no servers, no risk of data leaks.
Zero Re-compression — We only target the hidden text headers. Your image resolution, colors, and quality remain 100% original.
GPS & Privacy Audit — See exactly what information is hidden in your photos before you decide to wipe it clean.
No Data Consumption — Perfect for mobile users; once loaded, hugdot works offline without using your data plan to upload files.
ZIP Bundle Output — Save time by downloading all your cleaned photos in a single, organized ZIP file.

faq.q: Why is 'Batch' mode better on hugdot?
faq.a: Most tools make you upload one by one. hugdot uses your browser's power to clean 20 photos simultaneously, saving you time while keeping everything private.

faq.q: Does this tool work on smartphone photos?
faq.a: Yes! Smartphones are the primary source of GPS-tagged photos. hugdot is fully optimized for mobile and desktop browsers.

faq.q: What happens to the original pixels?
faq.a: Nothing. Unlike 'online optimizers' that re-encode your image, hugdot only slices out the metadata segments. Your pixels stay exactly as they were.

faq.q: Is it really safe to use for sensitive IDs?
faq.a: Absolutely. Because hugdot uses 'Client-Side' processing, the data never travels over the internet. It is physically impossible for us to see your photos.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Fast workflow for social media albums and marketplace listings
Removes headers without re-encoding, preserving 1:1 original quality
Military-grade privacy for sensitive documents and locations
Complete sanitization of all hidden tracking and technical data

troubleshooting: Already clean? Many social apps (like WhatsApp) strip metadata automatically. If hugdot says 'Clean', your photo is already safe to share.
Processing multiple files? If you're on a mobile device, keep the tab active to ensure your browser gives full CPU power to the batch engine.
ZIP file issues? Ensure you have enough storage space on your device, as hugdot packages the clean images in your local memory.
proTips: Always batch-clean your photos before posting on Reddit or public forums to prevent 'OSINT' tracking of your location.
Use the ZIP download feature to keep your 'Clean' versions organized and separate from your original high-res library.
If you need to hide a house number or face *visually*, use this tool first, then head over to our 'Image Blur' tool for total protection.
